old_claim.note: Laparoscopic Surgery provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Laparoscopic minimally invasive surgery became an important deployment context for teleoperated surgical robots, although the earliest robotic surgery was CT-guided neurosurgery.

source_shape.source_locator: Robotic surgery review sections on PUMA 560, teleoperation, and later minimally invasive robotic systems.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The review anchors robotic surgery to PUMA 560 in 1985 and describes subsequent robotic systems for laparoscopic and minimally invasive surgical procedures.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports an enabling laparoscopy edge while avoiding a hard prerequisite claim because the first robotic surgery was neurosurgical rather than laparoscopic.

ontology_before: Surgical robotics used a generic laparoscopy edge and a placeholder 1983 date.
ontology_after: Surgical robotics is anchored to 1985 robot-assisted neurosurgery while keeping laparoscopy as later deployment context for minimally invasive robotic systems.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: surgical robotics remains connected to laparoscopy, but only as an enabling deployment context.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to da Vinci-style laparoscopic teleoperation only.
A source showed the first surgical robot was a laparoscopic system rather than PUMA 560 neurosurgery.
The edge were upgraded to required and made all surgical robotics depend on laparoscopy.
short_reason: Laparoscopy matters for surgical robotics deployment but is not the origin of the whole field.
